steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the relationship between seismic monitoring and orbital imaging. The colon signals an explanation: seismic monitoring narrows the search area; orbital imaging then pinpoints the exact crater. Each method does what the other cannot.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — exhaustive: Means covering every possibility completely. Plug in: their roles are exhaustive — but the passage is about sequential, complementary steps, not total coverage. ✗
  • Choice B — redundant: Means unnecessarily repetitive. Plug in: their roles are redundant — but seismic locates roughly and orbital locates precisely; they do different jobs. ✗
  • Choice C — interchangeable: Means either could substitute for the other. Plug in: their roles are interchangeable — but you can't use orbital imaging to detect vibrations, nor seismic to pinpoint a crater precisely. ✗
  • Choice D — complementary: Means each supplies what the other lacks. Plug in: their roles are complementary — seismic gives rough location, orbital gives precision; together they form a complete method. ✓
That matches Choice D — complementary.
